task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. Empty lists, one-element lists, and two-element lists return None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly calculates the trimmed mean by removing one occurrence of the minimum and maximum values, handling edge cases for lists with less than three elements.
trace: ** I designed the function `trimmed_mean` to first check if the list length is less than 3, as per the requirements. If so, it returns `None`. Otherwise, it removes one occurrence of both the minimum and maximum values from the list using the `remove()` method. Finally, it calculates and returns the mean of the remaining elements as a float. This approach ensures that the function handles all edge cases as specified in the criteria.
